topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with a dull, heavy thud. I stared at the screen, my jaw tight. Every word swam before my eyes—the polite rejection, the mention of 'insufficient standing.' I reread the rejection letter three times, the words blurring into an infuriating pattern of dismissal. My hands, resting on the cool wooden desk, began to tremble slightly. I pushed my chair back so abruptly it scraped loudly against the linoleum floor. I stood up, pacing the small confines of my room, the silence amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. I wanted to smash the keyboard, to rip the paper in half, anything to shake off this crushing sense of finality.

[story 2]

"Denied." The single word echoed in the sterile quiet of the library study room. I crumpled the rejection packet into a tight ball in my fist, the paper protesting under the pressure. I let out a slow, ragged breath, the air catching painfully in my throat. My knuckles were white where I gripped the crumpled paper. I looked around the room, at the focused faces of my classmates diligently working, and felt a sharp, hot spike of anger bloom beneath my ribs. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound sharp enough to make someone look up. I began to pace the length of the room, my steps quick and uneven, unable to settle my weight anywhere.

[story 3]

I picked up the notification again, tracing the denial notice with a fingertip. The ink seemed to mock me. A low, guttural sound escaped my chest—a sound more like a growl than a word. I threw the paper onto the floor, watching it flutter momentarily before settling. My shoulders tensed up to my ears, rigid and inflexible. I walked to the window, pulling the curtain aside just enough to look out at the bustling campus below. The energy radiating from the crowd felt overwhelming, a stark contrast to the stagnant, heavy feeling settling in my own core. I clenched my fists so hard my nails dug crescents into my palms.
